question.text: The trustees of a mutual fund are required to meet at least:

answer.text: 6 times a year to monitor and review the activities of the fund
12 times a year, once every month without exception
4 times a year, once every quarter
2 times a year, once every half year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Trustees act in the interest of investors and supervise the AMC's activities. All major decisions taken by the AMC are subject to trustee approval. As per the regulatory framework, trustees must meet at least 6 times a year to monitor and review the fund's activities.
**The Trap:** Many candidates guess quarterly (4 times) or monthly (12 times). The correct answer is 6 times per year — approximately every two months. This ensures regular oversight without being impractically frequent.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. Trustee responsibilities and meeting frequency are common exam questions across MF certification exams.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The trustee oversight is a governance feature worth highlighting. It shows clients that there is an independent body regularly reviewing how their money is being managed, beyond just the AMC's internal controls.
